name: AWS Polly API
what_it_does: AWS Polly converts text to lifelike speech with 60+ voices across 30+ languages using standard and neural TTS engines — outputs MP3, OGG, or PCM audio in synchronous or asynchronous mode.
use_cases: Generating audio narration for agent-produced reports and content
Text-to-speech for IVR (interactive voice response) systems integrated with Amazon Connect
Creating voice responses for agent chatbots deployed via Alexa or Lex
Batch audio generation for e-learning content from text-based course materials
Real-time speech synthesis for accessibility features in agent-powered applications
not_for: Ultra-low latency TTS for real-time conversational AI (Cartesia or ElevenLabs are faster)
Voice cloning or custom voice training (Polly uses fixed voices, no custom models)
Teams not on AWS who need simpler API access (OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs are easier)
best_when: You're already on AWS and need reliable, affordable TTS with SSML support, good language coverage, and async large-batch audio generation.
avoid_when: You need ultra-realistic voice cloning, real-time streaming TTS <100ms latency, or are not on AWS infrastructure.

auth.notes: AWS SigV4 signing. IAM policies control SynthesizeSpeech and StartSpeechSynthesisTask actions. Async jobs write to S3 — requires S3 write permissions in addition to Polly permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Standard voices: 5M characters/month free for 12 months; Neural: 1M characters/month free
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard: $4.00 per 1M characters; Neural: $16.00 per 1M characters
pricing.notes: Character-based pricing. Neural TTS is 4x more expensive but significantly better quality. Long Form neural voices have separate pricing.

performance.rate_limits: 100 concurrent requests (SynthesizeSpeech); 100 concurrent synthesis tasks (async); soft limits raiseable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS standard errors with RequestId. InvalidSsmlException includes SSML syntax details. TextLengthExceededException clearly states limit. Async task status polling via GetSpeechSynthesisTask returns clear status codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SynthesizeSpeech is synchronous and returns audio directly — effectively idempotent for same input. Async StartSpeechSynthesisTask creates a new task each time — agents must track task IDs to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: SynthesizeSpeech input limit is 3,000 characters (billed characters) — agents must split long text with natural break points
SSML tags count toward character limit but differently than plain text — be precise about billing when mixing SSML and text
Async StartSpeechSynthesisTask saves to S3 — agents need both Polly and S3 write permissions, and must poll for completion
Neural voices are region-specific — some Neural voices are not available in all AWS regions
Response audio is in the HTTP response body as a stream — agents must buffer the entire response before playing or saving

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM role-based access. No PII stored by Polly — text is not retained after synthesis. TLS in transit. HIPAA eligible (spoken health content requires BAA with AWS). FedRAMP authorized.
